description: Of great value; highly esteemed and cherished. 'Thaemo' names the quality of preciousness—worth that calls for care, value that deserves protection.
sound_symbolism: The word holds carefully. 'Tha' /θa/ begins with gentle breath, reverence present. The vowel 'e' opens to recognition of worth. Then 'mo' grounds in deep caring, preciousness confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thaemo kana' (precious child). Related to 'thoepa' (sacred) but emphasizes personal value rather than communal reverence.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Recognizing preciousness awakens care—seeing the value of this moment, this being, this life.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Treating words as precious improves speech—when we value our utterances, we choose them more carefully.
